summary: Frontend testing of Firebase Firestore Manager. UI renders correctly: all 6 collections in sidebar, real-time READ from Firestore works (Languages has 3 docs, ArtistCollections has many docs). However, ALL WRITE OPERATIONS FAIL due to Firestore security rules - permission denied. This blocks Create, Edit, Delete and Bulk Upload (CSV/JSON) flows entirely. Empty-field client-side validation does work (toast appears).

integration_issues.issue: FirebaseError: Missing or insufficient permissions. Console error logged from ManualEntrySheet.handleSubmit when calling addDoc/updateDoc. Toast 'Failed to save document' should appear. Sheet remains open after failure (only closed on success), so user can retry, but no write is possible.

Not testable end-to-end because the underlying writeBatch / updateDoc / deleteDoc calls will hit the same Firestore permission-denied error. Read (onSnapshot) succeeds for all collections, confirming the project + config are correct.

action_items: FIX FIRESTORE SECURITY RULES: Project 'haribhajane-eb8b5' currently denies writes. Since the requirement is 'open access (no authentication)', update Firestore rules in Firebase Console to: rules_version='2'; service cloud.firestore { match /databases/{database}/documents { match /{document=**} { allow read, write: if true; } } }. Note: open rules are a security risk - recommend at minimum restricting by an API key check or App Check.
After rules are updated, re-test full CRUD + bulk CSV/JSON upload flows.
Consider closing the ManualEntrySheet on error too, OR explicitly show inline error inside sheet (currently only sonner toast surfaces it).
Bulk upload: papaparse with header:true may produce stray empty-string keys if CSV has trailing commas - validate before writing to Firestore.
critical_code_review_comments: DataTable.jsx renders all keys as columns from union of docs - large collections (e.g. ArtistCollections) cause very wide tables with horizontal scroll; consider column virtualization or a 'fields to show' selector.

ManualEntrySheet stores all field values as raw strings - numeric fields like 'ArtistID', 'Language_id' will be saved as strings, which will mismatch existing Firestore data types and break queries/sorting. Add optional type coercion (number/boolean/JSON).
BulkUploadDialog uses CSV header row blindly - no preview / column-mapping step; users can silently pollute collections with wrong schema.
Firebase config is hardcoded in /app/frontend/src/lib/firebase.js. Should be moved to env vars (REACT_APP_FIREBASE_*) to avoid leaking keys in source and to allow environment switching.
No confirmation dialog before delete - one accidental click on dropdown 'Delete' permanently removes a document.
DataTable: editing while another user mutates same doc has no conflict handling (Firestore lastWriteWins) - acceptable for this MVP but worth noting.

success_rate.frontend: 40% - reads work, all writes blocked by Firestore rules

test_credentials: N/A - open access app, no auth
seed_data_creation: Attempted to create TEST_ prefixed docs in 'Languages' collection; all writes rejected by Firestore so no test data was persisted (no cleanup needed).
context_for_next_testing_agent: App URL: https://collection-hub-97.preview.emergentagent.com. UI loads cleanly with sidebar of 6 collections and a real-time data table. The blocker is Firestore security rules: every write (addDoc/updateDoc/deleteDoc/writeBatch) returns 'Missing or insufficient permissions'. Reads (onSnapshot) work fine. Once rules are opened up in Firebase console, run CRUD + bulk JSON + bulk CSV upload tests on the 'Languages' collection (smallest, 3 docs) using TEST_ prefix and clean up after.
rca of the issue: Root cause: Firestore security rules for project 'haribhajane-eb8b5' deny client writes (default 'allow read, write: if false' or auth-only). The frontend uses Firebase Web SDK directly with no auth (anonymous), so every write request is rejected server-side. Reproduction: open app -> Languages -> Add Document -> fill one field -> Create -> console shows 'FirebaseError: Missing or insufficient permissions.' and sonner toast 'Failed to save document'. Mitigation: update Firestore rules to allow public writes for the listed 6 collections (or all, given the open-access requirement). Long-term: protect with App Check or move writes behind the backend with a service-account-signed admin SDK.
